reachability_artifacts: C-08 built sworn serve unsafe-bind matrix showing loopback default and non-loopback rejection until TLS, secret reference, and exact HTTPS origin all validate
360-pixel real-browser capture of login, pause, idempotent retry, stale If-Match conflict, authoritative paused state, focus restoration, and audit event
two-process contention capture proving only one command advances a shared revision and the other receives a non-mutating conflict
secret-canary scan over URL, browser storage, response, event, notification, and server log artifacts

effort_complexity.rationale: See spec.json; this security-critical slice requires Captain review of session, TLS, origin/CSRF, command authorization, and concurrency design before implementation.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent operator can trust a recoverable autonomous release loop, receive durable attention pages, and monitor or safely control it from a mobile web board.

positive_scenarios: A remote phone authenticates over TLS and pauses an active loop through one durable command.
A lost-response retry returns the original operation without applying the pause twice.
Two operators on the same revision receive one accepted operation and one visible conflict.
negative_scenarios: Remote bind fails before listen when TLS, authentication, or exact origin is absent.
Missing auth, origin, CSRF, idempotency, content type, or If-Match reaches no command handler.
No web handler writes status directly, spawns a loop CLI, or leaks authentication material.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: A single authenticated revisioned command path makes mobile intervention safe enough to use during long autonomous runs without introducing another state writer.

design_decisions.choice: Require TLS, a secure operator-secret reference, and an exact allowed HTTPS origin for any non-loopback bind.
options: TLS plus local authentication and exact origin
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified 2026-07-14 (D4): direct non-loopback self-hosting requires TLS, a secure secret reference, and one exact allowed HTTPS origin; network location alone is not identity.
design_decisions.rationale: Bearer/session authentication without transport confidentiality is not a safe remote-control boundary.

design_decisions.choice: Use short-lived HttpOnly sessions with session-bound CSRF and exact Origin checks for browser commands.
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified 2026-07-14 (D4): browser commands use short-lived Secure HttpOnly sessions, session-bound CSRF, and exact Origin validation rather than persistent script-readable or URL tokens.
design_decisions.rationale: It avoids persistent script-readable bearer material and gives ordinary browser requests an explicit cross-site defense.

design_decisions.choice: Require both durable idempotency and If-Match content revision at the command service, not only in HTTP middleware.
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified 2026-07-14 (D4): revision and durable idempotency are enforced by the local command service for every transport, including a future hosted relay.
design_decisions.rationale: The same safety must hold for CLI, MCP, web, retries, and concurrent processes.

design_decisions.choice: Render controls only from server-advertised allowed actions and wait for authoritative state before updating status.
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified 2026-07-14 (D4): clients render server-advertised allowed actions and wait for authoritative state/events instead of applying optimistic status mutations.
design_decisions.rationale: The UI remains an adapter and conflict/failure stays visible rather than being overwritten by optimistic presentation.
